name: ArchUnit
description: Java architecture test library for asserting packaging, layering, cyclic-dependency and naming rules in plain JUnit tests.

seo.title: ArchUnit — Test Automation / Device Cloud
seo.meta_description: ArchUnit: Java architecture test library for asserting packaging, layering, cyclic-dependency and naming rules in plain JUnit tests.

when_to_use: Enforce layer dependencies in hexagonal architecture
Prevent cyclic package dependencies in Java monorepo
Assert naming conventions for services and repositories
Fail CI builds when architecture rules are violated

pros: Enforces layering and package rules as real JUnit tests
Catches cyclic dependencies before they rot the codebase
Readable fluent DSL, reviewable alongside code
No runtime overhead, pure static bytecode analysis
cons: Rules can get verbose for nuanced architectures
Steep ramp for teams new to architecture testing
Slow on very large monorepos without filtering
review.verdict: The cleanest way to turn architecture decisions into executable guardrails on the JVM.
review.best_for: Java teams protecting hexagonal or layered architectures from gradual decay
review.not_for: Small projects or non-JVM stacks where lint rules already suffice
